hero.audience_line: For tradespeople, shops and small businesses
hero.description: I create or redesign your website so customers find your services, understand your offer and contact you easily. I guide you from start to finish, without jargon.
hero.microcopy: Free, no-obligation first conversation · Reply within one business day
hero.reel_hint: See the context and decisions behind it
hero.photo_alt: Minhaj Zubair, independent web developer based in Paris

problems.label: Your situation
problems.heading: Is your online presence costing you opportunities?
problems.intro: Before they call, customers often look you up online. Within seconds, they decide whether to trust you or keep looking at a competitor.

items.title: You are hard to find
items.description: When a customer searches for your service nearby, other businesses show up before yours.

items.title: Your website no longer reflects your work
items.description: An outdated, incomplete or missing website can create doubt, even after a word-of-mouth recommendation.

items.title: Mobile becomes a barrier
items.description: Services, menus, prices or contact details are hard to read on the phone your customers actually use.

items.title: You lose enquiries
items.description: If calling, booking or asking for a quote takes too much effort, prospects put the decision off.

items.title: Everything relies on word of mouth
items.description: Recommendations are valuable, but they do not always reassure prospects or reach new customers on their own.

problems.microcopy: Simply tell me what is happening. No commitment and no jargon.

manifesto.label: What changes
manifesto.text: You move from an online presence that leaves prospects unsure to a clear, credible and easy-to-use website. It presents your services, supports local visibility and makes every enquiry easier.

work.label: Selected work
work.heading: Projects built for real businesses.
work.intro: Every project starts with a specific situation, uses choices suited to the business and creates a path that informs, reassures or makes contact easier.
work.home_cta_text: See where each project started, what was delivered and what the website now makes easier for its users.
work.page_intro: Websites, web and mobile applications, custom-built from the first conversation to launch.
work.github_subtitle: More projects and source code are on GitHub.
work.closing_line: Your project could be the next one.
work.epilogue_note: What if you're looking at it right now?

labels.cta_title: Have a similar project in mind?
labels.cta_text: Tell me about your business. I'll tell you exactly what a website can do for it.

photographe.context: A photographer needed a portfolio worthy of her work: a gallery that showcases the images, a strong identity and a simple contact flow to turn visitors into bookings.
photographe.approach: A clean art direction that gives the photos all the space, an immersive responsive gallery, soft animations and calls to action present at every step, without ever stealing the show from the images.

toiture_artisan.context: A roofing craftsman wanted a website that inspires trust from the first second and generates quote requests, in a market where competitors are often invisible or outdated online.
toiture_artisan.approach: A structure entirely built for conversion: trust signals up front, services explained simply, quote requests reachable from every section and local search optimization.

luxa.context: Luxa was born from a personal observation: budgeting apps are often complex and discouraging. The goal: make money management simple, visual and part of the daily routine.
luxa.approach: A React Native mobile app with Supabase: secure authentication, real-time transaction tracking, customizable savings pockets and clear statistics. An interface designed to be opened every day.

unityvert.context: A landscape gardener wanted to grow his business with a real online presence: present his services, build trust and make getting in touch easy.
unityvert.approach: A warm, understated showcase website, well-structured services, Sanity-powered content so he stays autonomous, and a direct contact form. Launched on his own domain.

mets_merveilles.context: Mets & Merveilles is a recipe and menu management platform: create recipes, organize them by category and find them easily.
mets_merveilles.approach: A full-stack application: Next.js front end, Spring Boot API, PostgreSQL database and Clerk authentication. A project covering the whole chain, from data modeling to interface.

portfolio.context: This very website: the showcase of my work had to demonstrate what I sell, with polished design, controlled animations, strong performance and solid SEO.
portfolio.approach: Next.js with the App Router, GSAP and Framer Motion animations, FR/EN internationalization, complete technical SEO and careful loading optimization. The code is public on GitHub.

services.heading: Clear support, from visibility to ongoing care.
services.intro: Creation, redesign, local presence or maintenance: start with your problem, not a list of technologies.

about.heading: Behind the code, a person.
about.intro: Background, how I work, and what drives me every day.

about_teaser.label: Why work with me
about_teaser.heading: One point of contact who understands small businesses.
about_teaser.p1: I'm Minhaj, a freelance web developer based in the Paris region. From the first conversation to launch, you speak directly with the person designing your website and who knows your project.
about_teaser.p2: I turn your needs into simple decisions: what to show, what can wait and how to make contact easier. The quote is agreed before work begins, you approve key stages and I remain available after launch.
proofs: One contact from quote to launch
Clear explanations without unnecessary technical terms
A website shaped around your business goals

faq.label: Frequently asked questions
faq.heading: Clear answers before you decide
faq.intro: A website is an important investment. Here is what you should know before considering the next step.
faq.microcopy: One question is enough to start. Reply within one business day.

items.question: Will a website actually bring me customers?
items.answer: A website cannot guarantee customers on its own. It can make you easier to find, reassure people discovering your business and simplify calls or quote requests. We first define what it needs to achieve, then plan how to measure the enquiries it generates.

items.question: I already rely on word of mouth. Why change?
items.answer: A website does not replace word of mouth; it extends it. Someone who receives a recommendation will often search your name before calling. A clear site confirms they are in the right place, presents your services and gives them an easy way to contact you.

items.question: How much does a new website or redesign cost?
items.answer: The price depends on the number of pages, content, features and the condition of the current site. After an initial conversation, you receive a quote setting out the scope, price and what is included. You know what will be delivered before committing.

items.question: I do not have time to write all the content.
items.answer: You do not have to organize the whole website alone. I ask the right questions, help prioritize your services and tell you exactly which text, photos or details are needed. We build from what you already have.

items.question: I do not understand the technical side. Is that a problem?
items.answer: No. I handle technical decisions, launch and checks. You approve concrete things: content, appearance and your customers' journey, with explanations you can understand.

items.question: I have already had a bad experience with a provider.
items.answer: The project is split into visible stages: discovery, quote, content, proposal, build and launch. You know what is planned, follow progress and approve important moments. You speak directly with me, without a middleman.

items.question: Could I build the site myself with Wix or another tool?
items.answer: Yes. Those tools can suit a simple need if you have time to structure the content, customer journey, mobile version and visibility. My role is to handle that work, avoid choices that hinder enquiries and build a site suited to your business.

items.question: Will I own the website and be able to change it?
items.answer: The quote sets out ownership, hosting and how changes will work before the project begins. The site is built to remain usable and, depending on your needs, I can provide self-management tools or handle updates for you.

items.question: What happens after the website goes live?
items.answer: I check that everything works, help you get started and remain available for adjustments. If you prefer not to manage anything, maintenance and local visibility plans can continue the support.

maintenance.plans_footnote: No long-term commitment: cancel anytime with 30 days' written notice. Beyond the 30 minutes of included changes, extra work is billed at €70/hour, always approved with you before invoicing.

essential.label: The foundation
essential.name: Essential
essential.positioning: Your website stays online, secure and up to date. You don't have to think about it.
included: Website hosting and domain name management
Domain renewal and SSL certificate included
Email and WhatsApp support, handled within 72 business hours
30 min of minor changes included every month

visibility.label: Local visibility
visibility.name: Visibility
visibility.positioning: All the Essential maintenance, plus a Google profile that works for you.
included: Audit and optimization of your Google Business Profile
Updates to hours, services, photos and contact details
QR code and direct link to collect Google reviews
Message templates (SMS, WhatsApp, email) to request reviews
visibility.note: Authentic reviews only: I never publish, buy or solicit fake reviews.

Targeting adjustments: area, schedule, bids, budget
growth.note: The advertising budget is paid directly to Google, separately from the plan. Management is included for up to €1,000 of monthly ad spend.

about.title: About me

profile.title: Who I am
profile.paragraph1: Minhaj, 25, developer based in Paris. I started coding out of curiosity, now it's my job.
profile.paragraph2: My thing: taking an idea and turning it into something real. Whether it's a mobile app, a website or an API, I like seeing the result of my work.
profile.paragraph3: When I'm not coding, I'm at the gym or learning about real estate and entrepreneurship. I like building things, whether it's a project or my life.
profile.paragraph4: Currently available for new freelance projects. Got a project in mind? Let's talk.
